proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the de-identified features is associated with objective_response in univariate testing.
feature_030 contains usable information for predicting objective_response.

analyses.code: for each feature, chi-square (categorical) or Mann-Whitney (continuous) vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Strong univariate associations: feature_013 (response 34.6% if =0 vs 15.4% if =1), feature_008 (15.9% if =0 vs 36.1% if =1), feature_022 continuous (mean 28.2 in responders vs 47.2 in non-responders), feature_015, feature_001 (3-level gradient 28.2/22.8/18.2), feature_021, feature_020, feature_027, feature_024, feature_002 all p<1e-5. feature_014, feature_029, feature_005, feature_012, feature_009, feature_023, feature_019, feature_026, feature_032, feature_028, feature_007, feature_025, feature_010, feature_018, feature_006, feature_004, feature_017, feature_011, feature_003, feature_031, feature_016 are non-significant (p>0.1).
feature_030 is constant at 0 across all 50,000 patients; provides no information.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the binary features, only a subset have ~50/50 prevalence (and thus could plausibly be randomized treatments); most are skewed-prevalence patient characteristics.
feature_001 (3-level: 0/1/2) shows a monotonic decrease in objective_response across levels, consistent with a stage-like ordinal feature.

analyses.result_summary: Binary feature prevalence: feature_005=0.599, feature_013=0.550, feature_008=0.402, feature_011=0.304, feature_023=0.300, feature_015=0.201, feature_006=0.199, feature_019=0.150, feature_017=0.102, feature_021=0.100, feature_004=0.048, feature_027=0.031. None are exactly 50/50; the closest are feature_005, feature_013, feature_008. feature_010 has 5 ordinal levels (6-10), feature_001 has 3 levels (0/1/2).
Response rate by feature_001: 0->0.282, 1->0.228, 2->0.182. Monotonically decreasing (chi-square p=5.4e-72).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a full multivariable logistic regression of objective_response on all features, feature_008 has the strongest positive coefficient and feature_013 has the strongest negative coefficient.
After mutual adjustment, only a handful of features carry signal; the remaining features are noise covariates.

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logit: feature_008 beta=+1.20 (z=52.6), feature_013 beta=-1.13 (z=-45.4), feature_021 beta=-0.74 (z=-16.9), feature_015 beta=-0.69 (z=-22.1), feature_027 beta=-0.43 (z=-6.0), feature_001 beta=-0.33 (z=-19.3), feature_020 beta=-0.14 (z=-12.1), feature_022 beta=-0.10 (z=-5.5), feature_024 beta=-0.07 (z=-5.4), feature_002 beta=+0.06 (z=+5.6). All other features have |z|<2. Pseudo R^2=0.124.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_008 functions as a binary treatment: it has a strong positive adjusted effect on objective_response, balanced ~40% prevalence, and is the only feature with this profile; the other binaries are baseline patient characteristics.

feature_013 represents a poor-prognosis baseline characteristic (e.g., disease severity / metastatic burden / contraindication) rather than a competing treatment, given its strong negative response association.

analyses.code: for each binary b: logit(y ~ b + top_covariates)
analyses.result_summary: After adjustment for feature_001, feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, feature_027, feature_022, feature_020, feature_024, feature_002, only feature_008 carries a strong positive adjusted effect on response (beta=+1.20). All other binary features are either negative (feature_013, feature_021, feature_015, feature_027) or null (feature_006, feature_005, feature_023, feature_011, feature_017, feature_019, feature_004 all |z|<1.5). feature_008 is the unique candidate treatment.
feature_013=1 prevalence 55%; response rate 0.154 vs 0.346 in feature_013=0. Adjusted beta=-1.13. Consistent with disease-severity/poor-prognosis marker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_008 on objective_response is not uniform: it interacts negatively with feature_013, such that feature_013=1 substantially attenuates the treatment benefit.
Beyond feature_013, additional features (feature_015, feature_021, feature_027) also negatively interact with feature_008, each independently suppressing the treatment effect.

analyses.code: for each feature f: logit(y ~ feature_008 + f + feature_008*f + covariates); rank by interaction p-value.
analyses.result_summary: Interaction screen of feature_008 vs every other feature (with covariate adjustment): feature_008 x feature_013 beta=-2.05 (p~0), feature_008 x feature_015 beta=-1.35 (p=9e-104), feature_008 x feature_027 beta=-1.40 (p=2e-21), feature_008 x feature_021 beta=-1.23 (p=4e-46), feature_008 x feature_001 beta=+0.14 (p=2e-5; small modification). All other interactions p>0.04, mostly null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In stratified analysis, the absolute treatment effect of feature_008 (response rate difference) is large (>30 percentage points) when feature_013=0 but essentially zero (<2 percentage points) when feature_013=1.
The same on/off pattern holds for feature_015, feature_021, and feature_027: each modifier=1 collapses the treatment effect to near zero, while modifier=0 retains a large treatment benefit.

analyses.result_summary: feature_015=0: treat diff +0.251; feature_015=1: +0.008. feature_021=0: +0.225; feature_021=1: +0.006. feature_027=0: +0.209; feature_027=1: -0.022. feature_001=0: +0.207; feature_001=1: +0.201; feature_001=2: +0.198 (no on/off; just slight gradient).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, feature_027 are nearly independent of one another in this cohort (phi correlations near 0); they represent distinct patient characteristics rather than a single underlying construct.

analyses.code: for each pair (a,b) in modifiers: chi2_contingency; report phi.
analyses.result_summary: All pairwise phi between feature_013/015/021/027 are <=0.004 (essentially zero); chi-square p-values 0.38-1.00. The four modifiers are statistically independent of one another at baseline.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the subgroup defined by feature_013=0 (where treatment is effective overall), each remaining modifier (feature_015, feature_021, feature_027) further partitions patients into a treatment-responsive subgroup (modifier=0) and a non-responsive subgroup (modifier=1).

analyses.result_summary: Within feature_013=0: feature_015=0 -> treat diff +0.546, feature_015=1 -> +0.014. feature_021=0 -> +0.491, feature_021=1 -> -0.012. feature_027=0 -> +0.454, feature_027=1 -> -0.012. Each modifier independently abolishes the treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the subgroup defined by feature_013=1 (where treatment is already ineffective), the other modifiers (feature_015, feature_021, feature_027) provide no additional information about treatment effect: treatment-effect difference remains ~0 regardless of their value.

analyses.result_summary: Within feature_013=1: every modifier-by-treatment cell gives a treatment-effect difference between -0.031 and +0.022 (all near zero). Once feature_013=1, treatment is uniformly ineffective.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete predictive subgroup for feature_008 treatment benefit is the conjunction (feature_013=0) AND (feature_015=0) AND (feature_021=0) AND (feature_027=0). Within this subgroup, treatment increases objective_response rate by ~60 percentage points; outside it, treatment effect is essentially zero.

analyses.code: mask = (f013==0)&(f015==0)&(f021==0)&(f027==0); compare treatment effect inside vs outside
analyses.result_summary: All-zero subgroup (n=15,681; 31.4% of cohort): f008=1 rate 0.798 (n=6325) vs f008=0 rate 0.172 (n=9356); diff +0.626; chi-square p<1e-300; crude OR=18.98. Complement (n=34,319): f008=1 rate 0.161 vs f008=0 rate 0.153; diff +0.008. Treatment effect is concentrated almost entirely in the all-zero subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the all-modifiers-zero subgroup, no remaining feature (continuous or other binary) further modifies the treatment effect of feature_008 with a robust signal.

analyses.code: for each remaining feature f within mask: logit(y ~ feature_008 + f + feature_008*f); record interaction p.
analyses.result_summary: Within all-zero subgroup (n=15,681), interaction p-values for feature_008 vs every other feature: smallest are feature_005 (p=0.004) and feature_017 (p=0.033); next-smallest p=0.06. After Bonferroni correction across 26 features (alpha 0.05/26 ~= 0.002), no interaction is significant. The four-modifier subgroup definition is effectively complete.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_022 (range 0.1-3622, highly skewed) is a major prognostic continuous biomarker: its monotone increase corresponds to monotone decrease in objective_response.

In the full cohort, feature_022 appears to attenuate the treatment effect of feature_008 across quintiles, but this apparent modification is driven by confounding with the binary modifier subgroup; within the all-modifiers-zero subgroup, feature_022 does not modify the treatment effect.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate by feature_022 quintile (full cohort): Q1 0.323, Q2 0.293, Q3 0.244, Q4 0.189, Q5 0.151. Strong monotone decreasing relationship; Mann-Whitney p~2e-252.

analyses.code: treatment-effect difference by feature_022 quintile, full cohort vs subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Full cohort treatment-effect difference by feature_022 quintile: Q1 +0.378, Q2 +0.287, Q3 +0.223, Q4 +0.099, Q5 +0.025 (apparent modification). Within all-modifiers-zero subgroup, treatment-effect difference by feature_022 quintile: Q1 +0.640, Q2 +0.630, Q3 +0.598, Q4 +0.647, Q5 +0.610 (no modification). The apparent attenuation of treatment effect by feature_022 is fully explained by joint distribution with the four binary modifiers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A joint logistic regression with feature_008 main effect plus the four binary modifiers entered as interaction terms (and adjustment for feature_001, feature_022, feature_020, feature_024, feature_002) decomposes the treatment heterogeneity cleanly: each modifier contributes a separate negative interaction coefficient of similar magnitude to the main effect.

analyses.result_summary: Joint model: feature_008 main beta=+2.62 (z=70.3); f008 x feature_013 beta=-2.23 (z=-45.6); f008 x feature_015 beta=-1.64 (z=-25.2); f008 x feature_021 beta=-1.56 (z=-17.3); f008 x feature_027 beta=-1.66 (z=-10.9). Each modifier independently neutralizes ~60-85% of the log-odds treatment effect; if all four are =1, the predicted log-odds gain from treatment becomes near-zero or slightly negative. Pseudo R^2 rises from 0.124 (no interactions) to 0.178.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment assignment (feature_008) is balanced with respect to the modifier subgroup: prevalence of feature_008=1 is the same inside the all-modifiers-zero subgroup (~40%) as outside it (~40%), suggesting treatment was not preferentially given to the responsive subgroup.

FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT HETEROGENEITY HYPOTHESIS for objective_response: feature_008 (the candidate treatment) increases objective_response rate by ~63 percentage points (crude OR ~19) in patients with feature_013=0 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_021=0 AND feature_027=0; in any patient with at least one of those four modifiers equal to 1, the treatment effect is essentially nil (rate difference ~+1 percentage point, non-significant).

analyses.result_summary: Treatment prevalence inside all-zero subgroup: 0.4034. Outside: 0.4007. Difference 0.003; treatment is essentially balanced across the modifier-defined subgroups, ruling out simple confounding-by-indication for the headline result.

Within (feature_013=0 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_021=0 AND feature_027=0): n=15,681; among feature_008=1, 5046/6325=79.8% respond; among feature_008=0, 1610/9356=17.2% respond. Risk difference +0.626 (p<1e-300); OR=18.98. Outside this subgroup: n=34,319; feature_008=1 rate 0.161 vs feature_008=0 rate 0.153; risk difference +0.008 (n.s.). The complete subgroup definition for feature_008 treatment benefit is the conjunction of all four predicates: feature_013=0 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_021=0 AND feature_027=0.
